###############################################################################
# HABiesie — Presence Domain Contract
#
# Generated by: Full domain audit (PROMPT 4)
# Date:         2026-04-13
# Auditor:      Claude Code (claude-sonnet-4-6)
#
# Scope:
#   All 5 packages/presence/*.yaml files, plus cross-domain presence
#   consumption in context/, security/, lighting/, and alerts/
#
# This document is the ground-truth record of what the presence system
# actually does vs what it is designed to do. It identifies every
# deviation, missing wire, and broken entity chain.
###############################################################################

---

## Section 1: Domain Overview

The presence domain provides three layers of output to the rest of the system:

```
Layer 1: WHERE  — room-level AP location per family member
Layer 2: WHO    — is anyone home, arrival/departure events
Layer 3: TRUST  — is the person home a trusted family member or low-trust staff?
```

Layers 1 and 2 are functional. **Layer 3 (trust) is broken** — the automation
chain that should propagate maid/gardener schedules into the security trust
classification is severed. All three security sub-systems (classification,
correlation, threat scoring) silently ignore staff presence.

---

## Section 2: File Inventory

### packages/presence/ (5 files)

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `presence_helpers.yaml` | 84 | Arrival/departure booleans, last-event timestamps |
| `presence_core.yaml` | 245 | AP→room map, per-person location, RAW occupancy, groups |
| `presence_confidence.yaml` | 192 | Confidence scoring + binary occupied (per room) |
| `presence_boundary.yaml` | 609 | Boundary resolver (gate-based arrival/departure) |
| `presence_validation.yaml` | 114 | Unknown AP detection, AP sanity sensors |

**Note:** PRESENCE_CONTEXT.md documents `presence_templates.yaml`,
`presence_state.yaml`, and `presence_automations.yaml` — none of these exist.
The domain was reorganised; the context document is out of date.

### Context package (trust model lives here — architectural violation)

| File | Lines | Trust entities |
|---|---|---|
| `context/context_presence.yaml` | 249 | All trust booleans + derived binary sensors + maid/gardener schedules |

Trust model helpers/automations belong in `packages/presence/` per the
architecture rules. They currently live in `packages/context/`.

---

## Section 3: Sensor Catalog

### AP Location (Layer 1)

| Entity | Source | States |
|---|---|---|
| `sensor.ryan_ap_location` | `device_tracker.ryan_iphone_tracker` ap_mac | Bar / Office / Garage / Lounge / Bedroom Zone / Kitchen / Away / Disconnected |
| `sensor.vicky_ap_location` | `device_tracker.vicky_iphone_tracker` ap_mac | same |
| `sensor.luke_ap_location` | `device_tracker.luke_iphone_tracker` ap_mac | same |
| `sensor.tayla_ap_location` | `device_tracker.tayla_iphone_tracker` ap_mac | same |
| `sensor.unifi_ap_room_map` | static template attribute | `rooms` dict: MAC → room name |

AP→room mapping is hardcoded in `presence_core.yaml:22–34`. 7 MACs mapped
(Bar, Office, Garage, Lounge, Bedroom Zone, Kitchen×2). No fallback for
unmapped MACs — they produce 'Away'.

### Home/Away (Layer 2)

| Entity | Source | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `binary_sensor.anyone_connected_home` | `group.family_ap_locations` | ON if any family member NOT in Away/Disconnected/unavailable |
| `binary_sensor.anyone_home` | `group.family_people` (Mobile App) | ON if any person.* = home |
| `sensor.presence_active_rooms` | OR of occupied binary_sensors | Comma-separated list of occupied rooms |

Two parallel home-detection mechanisms exist: AP-based
(`anyone_connected_home`) and Mobile App-based (`anyone_home`). Security
uses `anyone_connected_home`. The two are not reconciled.

### Room Occupancy Pipeline

```
AP Location →  RAW occupied  →  Confidence Score  →  Binary Occupied (final)
               (binary)         (0–100, sensor)      (threshold ≥50, binary)
```

| Entity | Threshold / Delay Off |
|---|---|
| `binary_sensor.office_occupied` | ≥50 / 2 min |
| `binary_sensor.garage_occupied` | ≥50 / 2 min |
| `binary_sensor.living_areas_occupied` | ≥50 / 3 min |
| `binary_sensor.bedrooms_occupied` | ≥50 / 5 min |
| `binary_sensor.bar_occupied` | ≥50 / 5 min |

All occupied binary_sensors also gate on `input_boolean.system_startup = off`
to suppress false occupancy on restart.

### Trust Model (Layer 3)

| Entity | Type | Set by |
|---|---|---|
| `input_boolean.maid_on_site` | manual/auto | `maid_schedule_start/end` (Mon/Thu) |
| `input_boolean.gardener_on_site` | manual/auto | `gardener_schedule_start/end` (Sat) |
| `input_boolean.contractor_on_site` | manual only | Dashboard; reset at 18:00 daily |
| `input_boolean.staff_on_site_override` | manual only | Dashboard; clears derived sensors |
| `binary_sensor.staff_on_site` | derived | maid_on_site OR gardener_on_site (with override) |
| `binary_sensor.low_trust_present` | derived | staff_on_site OR contractor_on_site (with override) |
| `input_boolean.low_trust_present` | **LEGACY MANUAL** | Nothing (commented out in schedules) |
| `input_boolean.staff_on_site` | **LEGACY MANUAL** | Nothing (never set automatically) |
| `input_boolean.guest_mode` | manual | Dashboard; also set by `suppress_security_after_restart` |
| `input_boolean.holiday_mode` | manual | Dashboard |
| `input_boolean.entertaining_mode` | manual | Dashboard |
| `sensor.security_trust_mode` | derived | Reads `input_boolean.low_trust_present` and `input_boolean.staff_on_site` |
| `binary_sensor.security_low_trust_active` | derived | Reads `input_boolean.low_trust_present` OR `input_boolean.staff_on_site` |

---

## Section 4: AP Location Engine

### Room Map

```yaml
"68:D7:9A:79:E8:5A": "Bar"
"68:D7:9A:13:34:D7": "Office"
"68:D7:9A:13:32:07": "Garage"
"9C:05:D6:A8:7A:71": "Lounge"
"9C:05:D6:A8:5A:75": "Bedroom Zone"
"04:42:1A:45:20:88": "Kitchen"
"04:42:1A:45:20:80": "Kitchen"
```

Two Kitchen MACs are mapped (possibly two access points covering the kitchen
area or a replaced AP whose old MAC was not removed).

### AP→Location Logic

```
if ap_mac is in room_map  →  room_map[ap_mac]
if ap_mac not in room_map →  'Away'   (same as "not home" or "unknown AP")
if no ap_mac attribute    →  'Disconnected'
```

**Weakness:** 'Away' is produced both by "connected but not in a mapped zone"
and "not connected at all". This makes it impossible to distinguish a phone
connected to an unmapped AP from a phone that is genuinely away.
`binary_sensor.*_unknown_ap` sensors exist to surface unmapped AP connections,
but they use `device_tracker.ryan_iphone` (without `_tracker` suffix) for
some persons — see Section 10 for the entity inconsistency.

### RAW Occupancy `delay_off`

| Room | delay_off |
|---|---|
| Office | 15 s |
| Garage | 15 s |
| Living Areas | 30 s |
| Bedrooms | 30 s |
| Bar | 30 s |

These short delays mean the RAW binary flips off quickly when a phone roams.
The confidence layer adds a further 2–5 min `delay_off` on the final binary.

---

## Section 5: Arrival / Departure Detection

### Primary Resolver — `presence_boundary_resolver`

**Trigger:** `binary_sensor.main_gate_sensor → on`  
**Cooldown:** 90 seconds (prevents duplicate events from a single gate open)  
**Mode:** restart

**Flow:**
```
Gate opens
  ↓
25-second delay (AP settle)
  ↓
Pass 1: evaluate AP changes within window
  gate_time - 120s  to  gate_time + 600s
  ↓
Arrival:  any family member AP changed → not Away/Disconnected
Departure: any family member AP changed → Away/Disconnected
           + condition: binary_sensor.anyone_connected_home = off
  ↓
4-minute delay
  ↓
Pass 2: same logic (handles slow WiFi disconnect)
```

**Pass 2 departure gap:** Pass 2 departure branch does NOT check
`anyone_connected_home = off` — a departure will be logged even if another
family member is still connected. This is intentional (the condition was
removed, presumably because it blocked legitimate departures).

### Arrival Persistence

After detecting arrival:
- `input_text.last_arrival_person` set (cleared after 10s by `presence_marker_reset`)
- `input_datetime.last_arrival_time` set (persists)
- `input_boolean.arrival_detected` — **NEVER set by boundary resolver** (defined in helpers, but the resolver doesn't touch it)

**Bug:** `input_boolean.arrival_detected` is defined and referenced in
PRESENCE_CONTEXT.md but is never set by the boundary resolver.

### Door Correlation

Two additional automations provide entry/departure event flags:

| Automation | Trigger | Condition | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| `house_entry_event` | `front_door_sensor → on` | `front_security_gate_sensor` changed < 30s ago | `house_entry_event = on` (5 min) |
| `house_departure_event` | `front_security_gate_sensor → on` | `front_door_sensor` changed < 30s ago | `house_departure_event = on` (5 min) |

**Bug:** `house_departure_event` logbook.log message says "House **entry**
event recorded" — copy-paste error. Does not affect function.

### `presence_test_arrival` — Test Automation Left in Production

```yaml
- alias: Presence Test Arrival
  id: presence_test_arrival
  mode: single
  trigger:
    - trigger: state
      entity_id: binary_sensor.main_gate_sensor
      to: "on"
  action:
    - action: input_datetime.set_datetime
      target: input_datetime.last_arrival_time
```

**Issue:** No conditions, no cooldown, no AP check. Sets `last_arrival_time`
on every gate open event. Runs in parallel with the real boundary resolver.
Should be removed from production.

### `suppress_security_after_restart`

On HA start: enables `guest_mode` for 2 minutes. Suppresses security alerts
during startup entity reconstitution. Design is sound. Correct placement in
`presence_boundary.yaml`.

---

## Section 6: Confidence Scoring Model

### Formula (per room)

```
score = 0
if AP-based RAW occupied:   score += 60
if motion sensor active:    score += 30
if time window active:      score += 10
occupied = (score >= 50)
```

### Per-Room Time Windows

| Room | Active Hours | Score at AP only | Score at AP + time |
|---|---|---|---|
| Office | 06:00–23:00 | 60 | 70 |
| Garage | 06:00–23:00 | 60 | 70 |
| Living Areas | 06:00–23:00 | 60 | 70 |
| Bedrooms | 19:00–08:00 (night) | 60 | 70 |
| Bar | 12:00–23:59 or 00:00–02:00 | 60 | 70 |

**Maximum reachable score in current state: 70** (AP + time).
Motion sensors are all hardcoded to `state: "off"` (stubs).
The full 100-point scale is never used. The threshold of 50 is met solely by
the AP signal.

### Startup Guard

All confident `binary_sensor.*_occupied` check:
```jinja
{% set startup = is_state('input_boolean.system_startup','on') %}
{% if startup … %} false {% endif %}
```
`input_boolean.system_startup` is defined in `core/core_helpers.yaml` and
turned off 60 seconds after HA starts. This correctly prevents false occupancy
at restart.

### Motion Stub Stubs

All 7 motion sensor binary_sensors are defined in `presence_core.yaml` with:
```yaml
state: "off"
```
Comments document the design intent: "When real motion sensors are added,
ONLY these sensors should be updated — the confidence layer must not change."
This is good design. But until real sensors exist:
- The confidence layer is effectively AP-only
- Adding motion sensors will push scores to 90–100
- The 50% threshold will still be met — no recalibration needed

---

| `binary_sensor.boundary_permissive_window` | security_core.yaml (reads orphaned entities) | ❌ Always false |
| `input_boolean.low_trust_present` | context_presence.yaml (manual, never auto-set) | ❌ Always off |

## Section 8: Unknown AP Detection

`presence_validation.yaml` provides three sensors for UniFi AP sanity:

| Entity | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `sensor.unknown_unifi_ap_connections` | Count of active unmapped AP MACs |
| `sensor.unknown_unifi_ap_details` | String of unmapped MAC addresses |
| `binary_sensor.unknown_unifi_ap_detected` | ON if count > 0 |
| `binary_sensor.ryan_unknown_ap` | per-person |
| `binary_sensor.vicky_unknown_ap` | per-person |
| `binary_sensor.luke_unknown_ap` | per-person |
| `binary_sensor.tayla_unknown_ap` | per-person |

**Entity name inconsistency:** Per-room AP location sensors use
`device_tracker.*_iphone_tracker` (with `_tracker` suffix).
Unknown AP sensors use `device_tracker.*_iphone` (without suffix):

```yaml
# AP Location (presence_core.yaml):
state_attr('device_tracker.ryan_iphone_tracker', 'ap_mac')

# Unknown AP (presence_validation.yaml):
state_attr('device_tracker.ryan_iphone', 'ap_mac')
```

If these are the same physical tracker with two registered names, both work.
If they are different entities, the unknown AP sensor will never detect the
right MAC. The discrepancy should be verified.

---

## Section 9: Trigger Integrity Audit

| Automation | Trigger | `from:` | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| `property_entry_event` | `main_gate_sensor → on` | None | Low (gate sensor rarely unavailable) |
| `house_entry_event` | `front_door_sensor → on` | None | Low |
| `house_departure_event` | `front_security_gate_sensor → on` | None | Low |
| `presence_boundary_resolver` | `main_gate_sensor → on` | None | Low |
| `gate_open_too_long_permissive` | `main_gate_sensor → on for 5m` | None | Low |
| `maid_schedule_start` | `time at: input_datetime.maid_start` | N/A | ✅ Time trigger |
| `gardener_schedule_start` | `time at: input_datetime.gardener_start` | N/A | ✅ Time trigger |
| `sync_staff_state_on_startup` | `homeassistant: start` | N/A | ✅ Event trigger |
| `suppress_security_after_restart` | `homeassistant: start` | N/A | ✅ Event trigger |
| `presence_test_arrival` | `main_gate_sensor → on` | None | **⚠️ TEST AUTOMATION** |
| `presence_debug_logger` | multiple state changes | None | Low (logging only) |
| `presence_marker_reset` | `input_text.* state` | None | Low |

No critical `from:` violations for binary safety triggers. However
`presence_test_arrival` lacks any conditions and runs on every gate event —
it should be removed.

## Section 10: Bug Catalog

### BUG-P01 — `input_boolean.low_trust_present` never automatically set
**Severity:** Critical  
**File:** `context/context_presence.yaml`  
**Impact:** Security trust model silently broken for all scheduled staff visits

The maid/gardener schedule automations were originally written to set
`input_boolean.low_trust_present`. That code is now commented out:

```yaml
# context_presence.yaml
- alias: Maid Schedule Start
  action:
    - action: input_boolean.turn_on
      target:
        entity_id:
          - input_boolean.maid_on_site
          # - input_boolean.low_trust_present   ← COMMENTED OUT
```

`security_core.yaml` reads `input_boolean.low_trust_present` for both
`sensor.security_trust_mode` and `binary_sensor.security_low_trust_active`.
`security_logic.yaml` reads it for `sensor.security_event_classification` and
`sensor.security_correlation`.

Result: Maid/gardener on-site has NO effect on the security classification.
On maid days the system will classify her movement as a potential intruder
if family is away.

**Fix:** Change all security/lighting consumers to use the **derived**
`binary_sensor.low_trust_present` instead of `input_boolean.low_trust_present`.
Then remove the commented-out lines from schedule automations (not needed).

---

### BUG-P02 — `input_boolean.staff_on_site` legacy manual toggle pollutes trust model
**Severity:** High  
**File:** `security/security_core.yaml`

`sensor.security_trust_mode` produces "detected_staff" when
`input_boolean.staff_on_site = on`. This boolean is defined in
`context_presence.yaml` but is **never set by any automation**. It is a
manual toggle on the dashboard.

```yaml
# security_core.yaml — sensor.security_trust_mode
{% if is_state('input_boolean.low_trust_present','on') %}
  scheduled_staff
{% elif is_state('input_boolean.staff_on_site','on') %}   ← manual only
  detected_staff
{% else %}
  normal
{% endif %}
```

The "detected_staff" trust state is unreachable in normal operation.

**Fix:** Remove `input_boolean.staff_on_site` reference from trust mode
sensor. The derived `binary_sensor.staff_on_site` already covers this case.

---

### BUG-P03 — `binary_sensor.boundary_permissive_window` always `false`
**Severity:** High  
**File:** `security/security_core.yaml`  
**Impact:** Gate-open-too-long alert is silently disabled

```yaml
# security_core.yaml — boundary_permissive_window
{% set start = states('input_datetime.low_trust_start') %}
{% set end = states('input_datetime.low_trust_end') %}
…
{% if start not in ['unknown','unavailable',''] and … %}
  …
{% else %}
  false          ← always taken
{% endif %}
```

`input_datetime.low_trust_start` and `input_datetime.low_trust_end` were
commented out in `context_presence.yaml`. They appear in the entity registry
as **orphaned** (timestamp `1774875346`) and return 'unknown'.

`gate_open_too_long_permissive` checks:
```yaml
condition:
  - entity_id: binary_sensor.boundary_permissive_window
    state: "on"     ← always off → automation never fires
```

**Fix:** Rebuild `boundary_permissive_window` to use
`binary_sensor.low_trust_present OR input_boolean.guest_mode` directly,
removing the dependency on the deprecated datetime entities.

---

### BUG-P04 — Security automation trust conditions commented out ("for testing")
**Severity:** High  
**File:** `security/security_automations.yaml`  
**Impact:** Trust and presence checks are bypassed in all 4 intruder automations

All four intruder response automations have trust conditions disabled:

```yaml
# security_automations.yaml — repeated across grounds, rear, house, perimeter
condition:
  # disable for testing
  # - condition: state
  #   entity_id: binary_sensor.anyone_connected_home
  #   state: "off"
  # - condition: state
  #   entity_id: input_boolean.guest_mode
  #   state: "off"
  # - condition: state
  #   entity_id: input_boolean.staff_on_site
  #   state: "off"
```

The automations now fire on every high-confidence motion event regardless of
who is home. The correlation engine (`sensor.security_correlation`) provides
partial filtering, but it also has broken trust input (BUG-P01).

**Fix:** Re-enable conditions. Replace `input_boolean.staff_on_site` with
`binary_sensor.low_trust_present`.

---

### BUG-P05 — `holiday_mode` produces `intruder_high` state but no automation handles it
**Severity:** High  
**File:** `security/security_logic.yaml`, `security/security_automations.yaml`  
**Comment in security_automations.yaml line 35:** `holiday_mode: ❌ DOES NOTHING → BROKEN`

```yaml
# security_logic.yaml — sensor.security_correlation
{% elif grounds and holiday %}
  intruder_high
```

No automation triggers on `sensor.security_correlation = "intruder_high"`.
All automation triggers watch for `sensor.security_correlation = "intruder"`.
Holiday escalation is silently dropped.

**Fix:** Add trigger `to: "intruder_high"` to intruder response automations,
or add logic inside the existing automations to elevate severity when
`sensor.security_correlation = intruder_high`.

---

### BUG-P06 — `input_boolean.arrival_detected` never set by boundary resolver
**Severity:** Medium  
**File:** `presence/presence_helpers.yaml` (defined), `presence/presence_boundary.yaml` (not referenced)  
**Impact:** Any automation depending on `arrival_detected` never fires

`arrival_detected` is defined in helpers and documented in PRESENCE_CONTEXT.md
as the canonical arrival flag. The boundary resolver sets only
`input_text.last_arrival_person` and `input_datetime.last_arrival_time`. It
never sets `input_boolean.arrival_detected`.

**Fix:** Add `input_boolean.turn_on: arrival_detected` to arrival branch of
boundary resolver (with timed turn_off or reset on next gate event).

---

### BUG-P07 — `presence_test_arrival` test automation left in production
**Severity:** Medium  
**File:** `presence/presence_boundary.yaml:595`

```yaml
- alias: Presence Test Arrival
  id: presence_test_arrival
  mode: single
  trigger:
    - trigger: state
      entity_id: binary_sensor.main_gate_sensor
      to: "on"
  action:
    - action: input_datetime.set_datetime
      target:
        entity_id: input_datetime.last_arrival_time
      data:
        datetime: "{{ now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') }}"
```

No conditions. No AP correlation. Fires on every gate open and overwrites
`last_arrival_time` immediately, before the boundary resolver has had its
25-second settle time. Creates a race condition with the real resolver.

**Fix:** Remove from production.

---

### BUG-P08 — Unknown AP sensors use inconsistent device tracker entity
**Severity:** Medium  
**File:** `presence/presence_validation.yaml:35–45`

```yaml
# presence_validation.yaml (unknown AP detection):
state_attr('device_tracker.ryan_iphone', 'ap_mac')   ← no _tracker suffix

# presence_core.yaml (AP location):
state_attr('device_tracker.ryan_iphone_tracker', 'ap_mac')   ← _tracker suffix
```

If these are different registered entities with different `ap_mac` attributes,
the unknown AP count will always be zero regardless of actual AP connections.

**Fix:** Verify both entity names resolve to the same UniFi tracker. If not,
align `presence_validation.yaml` to use `_tracker` suffix.

---

### BUG-P09 — `house_departure_event` logbook message wrong
**Severity:** Low  
**File:** `presence/presence_boundary.yaml:200`

```yaml
- alias: "House: Departure Event"
  action:
    - action: logbook.log
      data:
        message: "House entry event recorded."   ← should be "departure"
```

Copy-paste bug. Logbook entry says "entry" for a departure event.

**Fix:** Change message to "House departure event recorded."

---

### BUG-P10 — Duplicate `staff` variable in `sensor.security_correlation`
**Severity:** Low  
**File:** `security/security_logic.yaml:155,158`

```jinja
{% set staff = is_state('input_boolean.low_trust_present','on') %}
{% set entertaining = is_state('input_boolean.entertaining_mode','on') %}
{% set holiday = is_state('input_boolean.holiday_mode','on') %}
{% set staff = is_state('input_boolean.low_trust_present','on') %}   ← duplicate
```

Second assignment is redundant. No functional impact.

**Fix:** Remove the duplicate line.

---

### BUG-P11 — Package architecture violation: trust model in `context/`
**Severity:** Low  
**File:** `context/context_presence.yaml`

All trust model helpers (`maid_on_site`, `gardener_on_site`, `staff_on_site`,
`low_trust_present`, schedule automations) live in `context/context_presence.yaml`.
Per the architecture rules they belong in `packages/presence/presence_helpers.yaml`
(helpers) and a `presence_automations.yaml` file (schedule automations).

This is a technical debt item, not a functional bug. Low urgency.

---

## Section 11: Trust Model Design

### Intended Architecture (Three-Entity Chain)

```
Schedule → input_boolean.maid_on_site      ─┐
           input_boolean.gardener_on_site  ─┤→ binary_sensor.staff_on_site ─┐
                                             │   (+ override)               │
           input_boolean.contractor_on_site ─────────────────────────────→  │
                                                                             ↓
                                                        binary_sensor.low_trust_present
                                                                             ↓
                                                        Security / Lighting / Alerts
```

**Override behavior:** `input_boolean.staff_on_site_override = on` forces both
`binary_sensor.staff_on_site` and `binary_sensor.low_trust_present` to `false`,
allowing family-only days to override the schedule.

### Current Broken State

```
Schedule → input_boolean.maid_on_site      ─┐
           input_boolean.gardener_on_site  ─┤→ binary_sensor.staff_on_site → (used by context_global only)
                                             │
                           ┌─────────────────┘ DERIVED CORRECTLY — but ignored by security

input_boolean.low_trust_present ← NEVER SET (schedule code commented out)
         ↓
Security (security_core, security_logic, lighting_departure) reads THIS
         ↓
Always "off" → Security always treats staff as intruders
```

### Summary of Entities to Migrate

| Entity | Current consumer | Required change |
|---|---|---|
| `input_boolean.low_trust_present` | security_core.yaml (x2), security_logic.yaml (x3), lighting_departure.yaml (x2) | Replace with `binary_sensor.low_trust_present` everywhere |
| `input_boolean.staff_on_site` | security_core.yaml (x2) | Replace with `binary_sensor.staff_on_site` or remove |

### Orphaned Entities to Clean Up

`input_datetime.low_trust_start` and `input_datetime.low_trust_end` are
orphaned in the entity registry (`orphaned_timestamp: 1774875346`). They can
be removed from the registry via Developer Tools → Entities.

### Migration Path (Priority Order)

1. **Replace `input_boolean.low_trust_present` with `binary_sensor.low_trust_present`** in:
   - `security/security_core.yaml` (lines 129, 152)
   - `security/security_logic.yaml` (lines 40, 155, 158)
   - `lighting/lighting_departure.yaml` (lines 44, 54)

2. **Replace `input_boolean.staff_on_site` with `binary_sensor.staff_on_site`** in:
   - `security/security_core.yaml` (lines 130, 154)

3. **Rebuild `boundary_permissive_window`** to use
   `binary_sensor.low_trust_present OR input_boolean.guest_mode` directly

4. **Re-enable security automation trust conditions** with correct entities

5. **Wire `holiday_mode` into intruder response automations**

6. **Remove `presence_test_arrival`** test automation

7. **Remove orphaned `input_datetime.low_trust_start/end`** from entity registry

8. **Wire `input_boolean.arrival_detected`** in boundary resolver

---

## Section 12: Confidence Scoring Model

### Model Summary

| Input | Weight | Current Status |
|---|---|---|
| AP-based RAW occupancy | +60 | ✅ Active |
| Motion sensor raw | +30 | ❌ All stubs → hardcoded `off` |
| Time-of-day window | +10 | ✅ Active (per-room window) |
| **Occupied threshold** | **≥ 50** | |

### Maximum Achievable Score (Current State)

- Without motion sensors: **70** (AP=60, time=10)
- With motion sensors active: **100** (AP=60, motion=30, time=10)

Occupied state requires ≥50. Currently, AP presence alone (score=60) is
sufficient to trigger occupied. Time window adds margin (score=70). Motion
sensors will not change whether a room is classified as occupied but will
increase confidence margin.

### Bedrooms Special Case

Bedrooms use an inverted time window (19:00–08:00) matching night use:
```jinja
{% if now().hour >= 19 or now().hour <= 8 %}
  {% set score = score + 10 %}
{% endif %}
```

Bedroom AP zone is "Bedroom Zone" — one zone covers all bedrooms. Individual
bedroom-level occupancy is not possible without physical sensors.

### Bar Special Case

Bar uses hours 12:00–23:59 or 00:00–02:00, reflecting typical social use:
```jinja
{% if now().hour >= 12 or now().hour <= 2 %}
  {% set score = score + 10 %}
{% endif %}
```

Note: `now().hour >= 12 or now().hour <= 2` is equivalent to
`now().hour >= 12` because `now().hour <= 2` is always false when
`now().hour >= 3`. This is a minor logic inefficiency (not a bug — the time
window is 12:00–02:00 which wraps midnight correctly with the OR pattern).

### Threshold Calibration Notes

- Threshold = 50 was set when motion sensors were intended to contribute 30
- With AP-only (max 70), the threshold is effectively "AP present = occupied"
  (since 60 > 50)
- When real motion sensors are added, score can reach 100 — threshold recalibration
  is not needed unless false positives emerge from motion-only occupancy

### `delay_off` Calibration

| Layer | Delay Off | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| RAW binary (AP-based) | 15–30 s | Allow for AP roaming transition |
| Binary occupied (confidence) | 2–5 min | Prevent brief exits from toggling lights |

The 5-minute delay on Bedrooms and Bar prevents false "unoccupied" during
brief hallway trips at night. This is well-calibrated for the use case.

---

## Section 13: Summary of Issues

| ID | Severity | Description | File |
|---|---|---|---|
| BUG-P01 | **Critical** | `input_boolean.low_trust_present` never auto-set — trust model broken for all staff visits | context_presence.yaml |
| BUG-P02 | **High** | `input_boolean.staff_on_site` legacy toggle pollutes trust sensors | security_core.yaml |
| BUG-P03 | **High** | `boundary_permissive_window` always false — gate-open-too-long alert permanently disabled | security_core.yaml |
| BUG-P04 | **High** | All 4 intruder automation trust conditions commented out "for testing" | security_automations.yaml |
| BUG-P05 | **High** | `holiday_mode` produces `intruder_high` state but no automation handles it | security_logic.yaml |
| BUG-P06 | **Medium** | `input_boolean.arrival_detected` never set by boundary resolver | presence_boundary.yaml |
| BUG-P07 | **Medium** | `presence_test_arrival` test automation in production, race-conditions real resolver | presence_boundary.yaml |
| BUG-P08 | **Medium** | Unknown AP sensors use `device_tracker.*_iphone` vs `*_iphone_tracker` inconsistency | presence_validation.yaml |
| BUG-P09 | **Low** | `house_departure_event` logbook message says "entry" not "departure" | presence_boundary.yaml |
| BUG-P10 | **Low** | Duplicate `staff` variable in `sensor.security_correlation` | security_logic.yaml |
| BUG-P11 | **Low** | Trust model lives in `context/` not `presence/` (architecture violation) | context_presence.yaml |

**Total: 11 issues — 1 critical, 4 high, 3 medium, 3 low**

The critical and high severity issues are all in the trust model chain.
Fixing BUG-P01 (replacing `input_boolean.low_trust_present` with
`binary_sensor.low_trust_present` everywhere) resolves BUG-P01 and BUG-P02
simultaneously and immediately activates the maid/gardener schedule for
security suppression.
